name: Ingrian
notes: Ingrian (ižoran keel), a Finnic (Uralic) language of Ingria, south of the Gulf of Finland. The modern Latin orthography is close to Finnish: the same vowel letters (a e i o u y ä ö, doubled for phonemic length) and consonants, plus ⟨š⟩ /ʃ/, ⟨ž⟩ /ʒ/ and ⟨z⟩ /z/ in the sibilant series and ⟨c⟩ /t͡s/. Consonant length is written by doubling (kk pp tt ss šš …). Ingrian has a phonemic palatalised consonant series (/tʲ dʲ nʲ lʲ sʲ rʲ/) triggered chiefly before ⟨i⟩ and by historical front vowels; it surfaces as the narrow ⟨ʲ⟩ diacritic and is not resolved from the plain orthography here, so palatalisation is under-generated. Diphthong offglides are written plain (⟨ai⟩ → /ɑi/) rather than with the non-syllabic mark. Vowel and consonant inventory and the doubled-length convention follow the Finnish base; the Finnic clade classification is shared.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Ingrian language — Phonology and Orthography
sources.notes: Consonant and vowel inventory (palatalised /tʲ dʲ nʲ lʲ sʲ rʲ/ series, sibilants /ʃ ʒ ts/), phonemic quantity, and the Finnish-like Latin orthography.

ancestors.notes: Finnic — the classification node this language sits under.

location.notes: Glottolog's representative point for Ingrian (Soikkola/Lower Luga area).
